hero.title: Archaeological Survey of India
hero.subtitle: Puri Circle, Odisha
hero.tagline: Preserving the rich cultural heritage and magnificent sandstone monuments of Odisha

hero_slider.slide1_sub: Discover the 13th-century Sun Temple at Konark, a globally celebrated UNESCO World Heritage Site shaped as a celestial stone chariot.
hero_slider.slide2_sub: Experience the architectural grandeur and ancient traditions of the historic 12th-century Shri Jagannath Temple Complex.
hero_slider.slide3_sub: Journey through Odisha’s premier excavated Buddhist universities, pristine monastic cells, and world-class site museums.
hero_slider.slide4_title: Preserving Our Past for the Future
hero_slider.slide4_sub: Witness the meticulous science, exploration, and field research behind protecting 40 centrally protected monuments across Odisha.

home.welcome_title: Puri Circle welcomes you to Odisha’s premier digital heritage gateway.
home.welcome_p1: As custodians of this region's historic treasures, we care for the sacred spires of Puri, the breathtaking architectural marvels of Konark, and dozens of unique regional temples and prehistoric sites.
home.welcome_p2: Whether you are planning your first trip from abroad, working on an academic paper, or rediscovering your hometown roots, we invite you to explore our ancient monuments, view interactive galleries, and join us in preserving our shared past.
home.ticker_1: Welcome to the official portal of Archaeological Survey of India, Puri Circle.
home.ticker_2: Puri Circle manages 40 centrally protected monuments and sites including Konark Sun Temple.
home.ticker_3: Visitor timing for all major monuments: 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
home.intro_p1: The Archaeological Survey of India, under the Ministry of Culture, Government of India, is the premier organization for archaeological research and the protection of the nation's cultural heritage. For the maintenance of ancient monuments, archaeological sites, and remains of national importance, the entire country is divided into 37 Circles and 1 Mini Circle.
home.intro_p2: The Puri Circle was established on November 9, 2022, following the division of the former Bhubaneswar Circle. It oversees 40 centrally protected monuments and sites across 15 districts in Odisha, including the World Heritage Sun Temple at Konark and three Archaeological Site Museums. To ensure the proper preservation of these sites, four Sub-Circles have been established in Cuttack, Dhenkanal, Konark, and Puri.

monuments.title: Centrally Protected Monuments
monuments.subtitle: Explore the 40 historical sites, temples, forts, and ruins under Puri Circle jurisdiction

visitor.title: Visitor Information
visitor.subtitle: Plan your visit to the monuments under Puri Circle
visitor.hours_desc: Most monuments are open daily from sunrise to sunset (approx. 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM). Site museums usually close on Fridays.
visitor.ticket_desc_1: World Heritage Monument (Konark Sun Temple): Indians - ₹40, Foreigners - ₹600.
visitor.ticket_desc_3: Entry is free for children under the age of 15 years.
visitor.museums_desc: Puri Circle operates 3 site museums: Konark Museum, Ratnagiri Museum, and Lalitagiri Museum, showcasing priceless antiquities excavated from the respective heritage sites.
visitor.rule_1: Do not deface or touch ancient carvings, sculptures, or structures.
visitor.rule_2: Littering inside the monument premises is strictly prohibited.
visitor.rule_3: Drone photography requires prior written permission from the Director General, ASI.
visitor.rule_4: Follow local guidelines regarding footwear and photography inside active temple shrines.

about_page.title: About Us
about_page.asi_desc: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI), an attached office under the Ministry of Culture, Government of India, is the premier national organization for archaeological research and the protection of our cultural heritage. Founded over a century and a half ago, the ASI regulates all archaeological activities, manages site museums, and maintains ancient monuments and archaeological remains of national importance across the country.
about_page.circle_desc: The Puri Circle was officially established on November 9, 2022, following the bifurcation of the erstwhile Bhubaneswar Circle. Our core mission is to carry out scientific conservation, preservation, exploration, excavation, and structural beautification of regional treasures. By focusing dedicated technical and administrative expertise on coastal, western, and northern Odisha, we ensure these irreplaceable historic sites are preserved to world-class standards while expanding public outreach and education.
about_page.jurisdiction_desc: Headed by the Superintending Archaeologist, the Puri Circle exercises administrative control over 40 centrally protected monuments and sites spread across 15 districts of Odisha. This expansive zone extends up to the borders of West Chhattisgarh, North Jharkhand, and North-East West Bengal, bounded to the east by the Bay of Bengal.
